target.title: fix(qa-lab): add live turn timeout env override
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

QA Lab live-frontier runs sometimes need a longer per-turn timeout than the built-in model-family floors allow, especially when validating slow frontier/provider routes or shared-runner conditions. Today operators can tune several QA Lab and transport timeouts with `OPENCLAW_QA_*_TIMEOUT_MS` env vars, but the live-frontier agent turn timeout itself is not adjustable without changing code.

This is intentionally separate from #96567. That PR fixes the `claude-cli/*` timeout bucket classification. This PR adds the operator escape hatch for slow live-frontier runs.

## Why This Change Was Made

The existing `resolveQaLiveTurnTimeoutMs` path already receives a caller fallback and then lets the provider policy enforce model-specific floors. This change adds `OPENCLAW_QA_LIVE_TURN_TIMEOUT_MS` at that fallback boundary instead of bypassing provider policy.

That means:

- A higher env value raises the floor for live-frontier turns.
- A lower env value does not accidentally undercut existing provider-specific floors like GPT-5 or Claude Opus.
- Invalid env values fall back to the existing timeout behavior.

## User Impact

QA Lab operators can run slow live-frontier scenarios with:

```bash
OPENCLAW_QA_LIVE_TURN_TIMEOUT_MS=420000 pnpm openclaw qa suite --provider-mode live-frontier ...
```

Existing runs without the env var are unchanged. Mock lanes keep using the caller fallback unless the env value raises that fallback.

## Evidence

Test Plan (run from repo root):

- `pnpm test extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.test.ts` - PASS, 1 file / 8 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - PASS.
- `pnpm format:docs:check docs/help/testing.md` - PASS, docs formatting clean.
- `git diff --check` - PASS.
- `~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/openclaw-pre-pr.sh --repo /root/projects/openclaw --plan-only --no-rebase` - PASS; mapped scope to `extensions`, `extensionTests`, and `docs`.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - PASS.

Known local tooling gap:

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-timeout.ts` was attempted twice, but the local wrapper stayed in `prepare-extension-package-boundary-artifacts.mjs` with no lint diagnostics; the attempts were stopped so no local check process remained. CI remains the canonical oxlint proof for this lane.

🤖 AI-assisted with GPT-5 Codex.

target.title: feat: add session stream mode command
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a shared `/stream` command (alias `/streaming`) for session-scoped preview streaming mode overrides without editing channel config or restarting the gateway.
- Persists the canonical mode on `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; accepted modes are `off`, `partial`, `block`, and `progress`. The command accepts `final` as a user-facing alias for canonical `off`.
- Extends the shared preview streaming resolver so session mode wins before channel/account config and default mode.
- Wires the override through Telegram, Discord, and Slack dispatch paths, and adds an explicit channel capability so unsupported channels refuse the command instead of falsely acknowledging a setting they will ignore.
- Intentionally out of scope: `/progress` / `streaming.preview.toolProgress` from #80989, Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix runtime wiring, and protocol/TUI patch support.

## Linked context

Closes #74077

Related #80989
Related #81009

Requested by the open issue and shaped by the prior #81009 failure mode: shared commands must either be honored everywhere they are accepted or explicitly refuse on unsupported surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `/stream` / `/streaming` can set a per-session preview streaming override for capable channels, the shared resolver uses the session override before channel config, and incapable channels do not persist a fake override.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, branch `clawsean/stream-mode-session-command`, rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`caab343461`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with production `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts` and `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts` imports; targeted Vitest; `pnpm tsgo:prod`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the production resolver/handler smoke:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module
{
  "unsupportedReply": "⚙️ /stream isn't supported on this channel yet.",
  "unsupportedStoredMode": null,
  "resolverSessionMode": "progress",
  "resolverWithoutSessionMode": "partial"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: unsupported channel handling refused `/stream` without writing `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; the shared resolver returned `progress` when the session override was present and fell back to channel config `partial` without it.
- What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack UI behavior from a deployed gateway branch; Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix are intentionally unsupported by the new capability gate in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this source checkout was not deployed to the live gateway for UI proof in this contribution pass, so live channel screenshots are left for maintainer/requested follow-up. Local runtime/unit coverage covers command registration, set/status/reset, unsupported-channel refusal/no persist, resolver session precedence, and Telegram/Discord/Slack helper precedence.
- Before evidence: current `main` has no `/stream` command/session override; prior related PR #81009 demonstrated the failure mode where a shared command acknowledged an override only one channel honored.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/discord/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/stream-mode.test.ts` — passed, 5 shards / 88 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
- `pnpm check:test-types`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` and `git diff --check` — passed.
- Earlier `node scripts/check.mjs --timed` on the first branch head was blocked by stale `npm-shrinkwrap.json` guard before later gates; this PR does not change dependencies.
- Earlier local `check-changed` child path on the first branch head passed core prod typecheck, then hit existing `packages/gateway-protocol/src/native-protocol-levels.guard.test.ts` type errors. `pnpm check:test-types` now passes locally on the current base.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: shared command exposure vs channel runtime honoring, the same class of issue that closed #81009.

Mitigation: this PR introduces `previewStreamingSessionOverride` as an explicit channel capability, marks only Telegram/Discord/Slack as capable, and gates `handleStreamCommand` so unsupported channels reply that `/stream` is unsupported and do not persist an override.

## Current review state

- Author implementation complete and pushed as one commit.
- Awaiting maintainer/ClawSweeper review on the session override storage shape, the capability gate, and whether Telegram/Discord/Slack is the right MVP channel set.
- Known caveat: live UI proof is not included yet; local runtime/unit coverage and a runtime resolver/handler smoke transcript are included.

target.title: fix(trajectory): preserve usage in truncated events
target.body:
Fixes #96804.

## What Problem This Solves
Oversized `model.completed` trajectory rows currently replace the entire `data` block with a truncation stub. When the size pressure comes from `messagesSnapshot`, that also discards small accounting fields such as `data.usage`, so heavy turns can appear to have zero token usage downstream.

## Why This Change Was Made
The event limit should still protect trajectory files from very large payloads, but truncation should not throw away tiny structured fields that are important for accounting. This change keeps the existing per-event byte cap, emits `droppedFields` for omitted data, and opportunistically preserves `usage` and `promptCache` when they fit inside the bounded row.

ClawSweeper pointed out the Codex app-server recorder has the same event-level truncation shape. This update applies the same bounded preserved-field behavior there and adds a regression test through `recordCodexTrajectoryCompletion`, so both trajectory recorders preserve accounting-critical usage fields.

## User Impact
Usage and cost consumers can continue reading `model.completed.data.usage` for heavy turns even when the runtime has to omit large snapshot payloads. Large fields such as `messagesSnapshot` remain dropped, so the trajectory size budget is still enforced.

## Real Behavior Proof
I ran a real recorder proof against the patched code using both runtime recorder paths. The script created temporary trajectory JSONL files through `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and `createCodexTrajectoryRecorder`, recorded oversized `model.completed` events with large `messagesSnapshot` arrays, flushed them to disk, then parsed the persisted events.

```json
{
  "proof": "trajectory oversized model.completed preserves usage",
  "core": {
    "type": "model.completed",
    "truncated": true,
    "reason": "trajectory-event-size-limit",
    "hasUsage": true,
    "usageTotal": 724402,
    "hasPromptCache": true,
    "hasMessagesSnapshot": false,
    "droppedFields": ["messagesSnapshot"],
    "bytes": 667
  },
  "codex": {
    "type": "model.completed",
    "truncated": true,
    "reason": "trajectory-event-size-limit",
    "hasUsage": true,
    "usageTotal": 724402,
    "hasPromptCache": false,
    "hasMessagesSnapshot": false,
    "droppedFields": [
      "threadId",
      "turnId",
      "timedOut",
      "yieldDetected",
      "aborted",
      "promptError",
      "assistantTexts",
      "messagesSnapshot"
    ],
    "bytes": 753
  }
}
```

## Evidence
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts`
- `pnpm oxfmt --check src/trajectory/runtime.ts src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: fix(session-memory): skip transcript-only assistant messages in getRecentSessionContent
target.body:
## Summary

Session-memory hook produces duplicate assistant lines in memory files when the model uses thinking/reasoning, because the session JSONL stores both the raw assistant message (with thinking blocks + text) and a transcript-only `delivery-mirror` copy of the same response, and `getRecentSessionContent()` reads both as valid text messages.

- Problem: `getRecentSessionContent()` → `extractTextMessageContent()` extracts text from every message entry. The session JSONL contains both the original assistant response and a `delivery-mirror`/`gateway-injected` copy with identical text, producing duplicate `assistant: ...` lines.
- Solution: In `getRecentSessionContent()`, skip delivery-mirror rows only when their text duplicates the preceding assistant text. Reset `lastAssistantText` on user messages so cross-turn delivery-mirror rows echoing old assistant text are not silently filtered. Delivery-mirror rows with unique visible content (e.g., message-tool replies) are preserved.
- What changed: `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts` — track `lastAssistantText`, reset on user messages. `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` — add message-tool preservation and cross-turn delivery-mirror regression tests.
- What did NOT change: Storage layer (session JSONL format, `message_end` persistence), handler.ts (hook lifecycle), streaming subscriber, test coverage for existing behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #92563
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When using a model with thinking/reasoning (e.g., DeepSeek), the session JSONL persists two copies of each assistant response:
1. The raw version with `thinking` + `text` content blocks
2. A cleaned version (`delivery-mirror` model) containing only the `text` block

The session-memory hook's `getRecentSessionContent()` processes all JSONL entries equally via `extractTextMessageContent()`, which extracts the first `type: "text"` block from each. Both entries produce the same text, causing every assistant line to appear twice in generated memory files (e.g., after `/new` or `/reset`).

This makes session memory files harder to read and wastes context budget when recalled by the agent.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `getRecentSessionContent()` no longer emits duplicate assistant lines from delivery-mirror transcript entries, while preserving standalone gateway-injected replies.
- Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node 22.11.0, branch `fix/session-memory-dup-assistant-92563`, OpenClaw `c58e1abf6a` (origin/main)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```
  node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
  import fs from "node:fs";
  import path from "node:path";
  import os from "node:os";
  import { getRecentSessionContent } from "./src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts";

  const dir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "proof-92563-"));
  const sessionFile = path.join(dir, "session.jsonl");

  const lines = [
    { type: "session", id: "test-session" },
    { type: "message", message: { role: "user", content: "What is 2+2?" } },
    // Raw assistant with thinking blocks
    { type: "message", message: { role: "assistant", provider: "openclaw", model: "claude", content: [{ type: "thinking", text: "..." }, { type: "text", text: "2+2 = 4" }] } },
    // delivery-mirror: cleaned copy
    { type: "message", message: { role: "assistant", provider: "openclaw", model: "delivery-mirror", content: [{ type: "text", text: "2+2 = 4" }] } },
    { type: "message", message: { role: "user", content: "And 3+3?" } },
    // Second raw assistant
    { type: "message", message: { role: "assistant", provider: "openclaw", model: "claude", content: [{ type: "thinking", text: "..." }, { type: "text", text: "3+3 = 6" }] } },
    // delivery-mirror
    { type: "message", message: { role: "assistant", provider: "openclaw", model: "delivery-mirror", content: [{ type: "text", text: "3+3 = 6" }] } },
    // gateway-injected with distinct visible content (should be PRESERVED after narrow fix)
    { type: "message", message: { role: "assistant", provider: "openclaw", model: "gateway-injected", content: [{ type: "text", text: "standalone gateway injected reply" }] } },
  ];

  fs.writeFileSync(sessionFile, lines.map(l => JSON.stringify(l)).join("\n") + "\n", "utf-8");
  const content = await getRecentSessionContent(sessionFile, 20);
  console.log(content);
  fs.rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
  NODE
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  Terminal console output from running the reproduction script with the patch applied:

  ```console
  $ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
  import { getRecentSessionContent } from './src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts';
  ...
  NODE
  user: What is 2+2?
  assistant: 2+2 = 4
  user: And 3+3?
  assistant: 3+3 = 6
  assistant: standalone gateway injected reply
  ```

  **Input matrix** (8 JSONL entries → 5 output lines):

  | JSONL entry | Extracted? | Reason |
  |-------------|-----------|--------|
  | user "What is 2+2?" | Included | Normal user message |
  | assistant (claude) "2+2 = 4" + thinking | Included | Normal assistant reply |
  | assistant (delivery-mirror) "2+2 = 4" | Skipped | Transcript-only internal message |
  | user "And 3+3?" | Included | Normal user message |
  | assistant (claude) "3+3 = 6" + thinking | Included | Normal assistant reply |
  | assistant (delivery-mirror) "3+3 = 6" | Skipped | Transcript-only internal message |
  | assistant (gateway-injected) "standalone gateway injected reply" | Preserved | Standalone visible assistant reply (delivery-mirror is filtered; non-duplicate gateway-injected preserved) |

  **Vitest regression test result** (hooks test suite including the new gateway-injected preservation test):

  ```
   Test Files  19 passed (19)
        Tests  195 passed (195)
  ```

- **Updated test suite (commit 79a20accca):**
  ```
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  26 passed (26)
  ```

- **Rebased against upstream/main** (which added `sanitizeSessionMemoryTranscriptText`). Combined fix preserves both delivery-mirror dedup and model artifact sanitization.

- **Message-tool scenario verified:** A delivery-mirror row with text different from any preceding assistant row is correctly preserved.

- **E2E proof (4 scenarios):**

  ```
  ========================================================================
  PR #94401  E2E Proof
  ========================================================================

  --- Scenario A: Thinking duplicate (DM text matches raw asst) ---
    Output: user: What is 2+2? | assistant: 2+2 = 4
    assistant lines: 1 (expect 1)
    PASS - duplicate filtered

  --- Scenario B: Message-tool DM with unique text ---
    Output: user: Turn on lights | assistant: Lights turned on
    assistant lines: 1 (expect 1)
    PASS - message-tool reply preserved

  --- Scenario C: Gateway-injected standalone reply ---
    Output: user: Hello | assistant: Hi | assistant: Standalone gateway reply
    PASS - gateway-injected preserved

  --- Scenario D: Full mix ---
    Output:
      user: What is 2+2?
      assistant: 2+2 = 4
      user: Turn on lights
      assistant: Lights turned on
      assistant: Standalone gateway reply
    PASS - all expected lines present

  --- Summary ---
    PASS - Thinking duplicates filtered
    PASS - Message-tool DM preserved
    PASS - Gateway-injected preserved
    PASS - Full mix correct
  ========================================================================
  ```

- **Cross-turn delivery-mirror fix (commit `faaa8211b1`):**
  When a delivery-mirror row echoes a previous turn's assistant text after a user message, the fix ensures it is preserved — not incorrectly filtered. This addresses the edge case where `lastAssistantText` carried stale state across turns.

  ```console
  $ node --import tsx -e "
  import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
  import os from 'node:os'; import path from 'node:path';
  import { getRecentSessionContent } from './src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/transcript.ts';
  ...
  "
  === Session JSONL ===
  {"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"Your number is 123-4567"}}
  {"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","provider":"openclaw","model":"delivery-mirror","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Your number is 123-4567"}]}}
  {"type":"message","message":{"role":"user","content":"I changed it to 987-6543"}}
  {"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","provider":"openclaw","model":"delivery-mirror","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Your number is 123-4567"}]}}

  === getRecentSessionContent output ===
  assistant: Your number is 123-4567
  user: I changed it to 987-6543
  assistant: Your number is 123-4567

  === Assistant lines count === 2
  PASS: Cross-turn delivery-mirror preserved correctly
  ```

  **Updated test results** (hooks CI-like shard):
  ```
   Test Files  21 passed (21)
        Tests  203 passed (203)
  ```

  **`oxlint`**: exit 0, no warnings.

- **Format check:** `oxfmt --check` — all matched files use the correct format.

- Observed result after fix:
  1. 8 JSONL entries → 5 output lines (2 user + 3 unique assistant lines: delivery-mirror duplicates filtered, gateway-injected standalone preserved)
  2. Each unique assistant response appears exactly once
  3. Gateway-injected standalone entries are preserved (only delivery-mirror duplicates are filtered)
  4. Cross-turn delivery-mirror rows that echo older assistant text are preserved (no false filtering across user turns)
  5. Existing behavior for user messages, normal assistant messages, and command messages is preserved
- What was not tested: Live OpenClaw agent session with a thinking model (requires provider API key and gateway). The unit test in `handler.test.ts` covers the delivery-mirror filtering, message-tool preservation, and cross-turn boundary scenarios with mock data.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The session JSONL stores two entries per assistant response when thinking is used: the raw message (with `thinking` + `text` blocks) and a `delivery-mirror` message (cleaned text-only copy, `parent` pointing to the raw entry). `extractTextMessageContent()` reads the first `type: "text"` block from each, producing identical `assistant: <text>` lines.

Provenance:
- `introduced by`: `faba508fe0` (feat: add internal hooks system) — initial `getRecentSessionContent`
- `made visible by`: `bb46b79d3c1` (#85341, internalize agent runtime) — delivery-mirror entries appear alongside raw messages
- `carried forward by`: `c109a7623b` (refactor lint) — `extractTextMessageContent` extracted as standalone function
- Confidence: clear

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Regression tests added in `handler.test.ts`:
  - `"filters delivery-mirror duplicates but preserves standalone gateway-injected assistant rows (fixes #92563)"` — 38 lines, covers delivery-mirror duplicate filtering and gateway-injected preservation.
  - `"preserves delivery-mirror with unique text when no raw assistant precedes it (message-tool scenario)"` — verifies message-tool replies are not lost.
  - `"preserves delivery-mirror after user turn even when mirroring older assistant text"` — verifies cross-turn delivery-mirror is not incorrectly filtered when text matches a previous turn's assistant content.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Session memory files will no longer contain duplicate assistant lines when using thinking/reasoning models. The same unique text appears once per assistant response, making memory files shorter and more readable.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
Before fix:
  JSONL: [raw user msg] [raw asst (thinking+text)] [delivery-mirror (text)] → assistant: "text" + assistant: "text"  ← DUPLICATE

After fix:
  JSONL: [raw user msg] [raw asst (thinking+text)] [delivery-mirror (text)] → assistant: "text"  ← ONE
```

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x64
- Runtime: Node 22.11.0
- Branch: `fix/session-memory-dup-assistant-92563`

### Steps

1. Create a mock session JSONL with both raw assistant entries and delivery-mirror entries (see proof above)
2. Call `getRecentSessionContent()` on the file
3. Observe that each unique assistant text appears exactly once

### Expected

No duplicate `assistant:` lines in the extracted content.

### Actual

Before fix: duplicate assistant lines. After fix: each assistant response appears once.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Mock session JSONL with thinking-model structure, delivery-mirror entries, gateway-injected entries, interleaved user messages
- Edge cases checked: Multiple delivery-mirror entries for the same text, mixed thinking models, gateway-injected messages, cross-turn delivery-mirror echoing old assistant text after user message
- What you did NOT verify: Live OpenClaw agent session with a real thinking model (requires API key and gateway)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only reduces output, never adds.
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — filtering by `model` at the consumer boundary is the correct layer. Delivery-mirror messages are internal agent-bookkeeping artifacts and should not appear in user-visible session memory. Gateway-injected entries with non-duplicate visible content are preserved. This follows the narrower `isOpenClawDeliveryMirrorAssistantMessage()` pattern.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The fix is bounded and the storage layer (session JSONL) correctly preserves internal bookkeeping for replay/compaction purposes.
- **Alternative considered**: Text-level deduplication (skip consecutive identical assistant text). Initially rejected because it could suppress legitimate repeated assistant replies from different turns. The final fix combines text comparison with user-turn tracking: `lastAssistantText` is reset on user messages, so cross-turn duplicates are preserved while same-turn duplicates are still filtered.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: The AI analyzed the bug provenance (git log -S, git blame), designed the fix approach (semantic delivery-mirror filtering over text-level dedup), implemented the change, and produced this PR body.

## Risks and Mitigations

**Risk**: A legitimate `openclaw` provider assistant message could be misidentified as transcript-only.  
**Mitigation**: The filter only matches `delivery-mirror` — this is a reserved internal model value. The text comparison ensures only actual duplicates are skipped, not unique delivery-mirror content from message-tool sends.

**Risk**: Future internal agent messages with different model names could reintroduce duplicates.  
**Mitigation**: The text-based skip is model-agnostic — any future message type that produces identical consecutive assistant text will also be deduplicated.

---

Fixes #92563

commands.author: clawSean

target.title: feat: configurable default agent ID (escape the 'main' prison) 🦞
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for overriding the hardcoded `"main"` fallback agent ID, so users with a single custom-named agent no longer get a ghost `agents/main/` default path/session identity when no `agents.list` is configured.

## The Problem

`DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` falls back to `"main"`. Some resolution paths run from partial config or before a full multi-agent list exists, so a setup that intends a custom default agent can still resolve the main session key and default agent directory as `main`.

## Solution

Two complementary override mechanisms:

### 1. Config key: `agents.defaultAgentId`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaultAgentId": "maine-lobster"
  }
}
```

### 2. Env var: `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`

```bash
export OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID=maine-lobster
```

### Priority (highest → lowest)

1. `agents.list[].default: true` (explicit default agent)
2. `agents.list[0]` (first configured agent)
3. `agents.defaultAgentId` config key
4. `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` env var
5. Hardcoded `"main"` (backward compatible)

## Changes

- Adds `agents.defaultAgentId` to the config schema/types and generated docs baseline.
- Updates `resolveDefaultAgentId()`, `listAgentIds()`, `resolveDefaultAgentDir()`, and main-session key resolution to honor config/env fallback values.
- Keeps populated `agents.list` behavior unchanged: list default/first entry still wins.
- Updates provider auth/default-agent directory resolution to use the effective default agent.
- Adds regression tests for config default, env fallback, default dir, and main-session key behavior.

## Testing

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope-config.test.ts src/config/sessions/main-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-key.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` → merge-clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A config-only default agent ID should stop OpenClaw from falling back to the hardcoded `main` agent for the default agent directory and main session key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source directly with Node/tsx in `/root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-pr-acpx/pr-51762`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveDefaultAgentId, resolveDefaultAgentDir } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts';
import { resolveMainSessionKey } from './src/config/sessions/main-session.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaultAgentId: 'Ops Team' }, session: { mainKey: 'main' } };
const env = { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: '/tmp/openclaw-real-proof' };
console.log('defaultAgentId=' + resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg, env));
console.log('defaultAgentDir=' + resolveDefaultAgentDir(cfg, env));
console.log('mainSessionKey=' + resolveMainSessionKey(cfg, env));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output copied from the patched real source command above:

```console
defaultAgentId=ops-team
defaultAgentDir=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent
mainSessionKey=agent:ops-team:main
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `agents.defaultAgentId: "Ops Team"` is normalized to `ops-team`, the default agent directory resolves under `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent`, and the main session key resolves to `agent:ops-team:main` instead of `main`.
- **What was not tested**: No browser/UI flow was tested; this PR changes config/session/agent resolution behavior and was verified through the live runtime resolver functions plus the targeted regression/check suite above.

Fixes #17477 🦞

target.title: fix(status): wrap buildStatusText with try-catch and protect lazy dynamic imports
target.body:
## Summary

`buildStatusText` can silently fail on LINE/webhook callers when a lazy dynamic import fails in cold paths. The PR wraps `buildStatusReply` and `buildStatusText` in try-catch fallback guards, replaces status lazy import promise caches with a retry-on-rejection `lazyStatusModule` loader, and makes `BuildStatusTextParams.sessionKey` optional.

Fixes #94626.

## Changes since last review

**P1 fix — type contract alignment**: Changed `resolveStatusHarnessId` param `sessionKey` from `string` to `string?` so it matches `BuildStatusTextParams.sessionKey?: string`. The downstream `selectAgentHarness` already accepts `sessionKey?: string`, so this is a pure type-level alignment with zero runtime impact.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: `/status` no longer drops the reply when a lazy dynamic import or runtime data collection fails.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ fb440807 (PR branch e10aa57e)

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/status/status-text.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  7 passed (7)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  30 passed (30)
```

Test names from status-text.test.ts:
- "returns a string (not throws) even when invoked without optional session data"
- "does not throw when called without a sessionKey"

These exercise the try-catch boundary that was previously letting LINE callers drop replies silently.

**Observed result after fix**: All 37 tests pass. The try-catch boundary catches failures and returns a non-empty string. The lazyStatusModule loader clears rejected caches so transient import failures heal on the next call.

**What was not tested**: A live LINE channel /status call after a real import failure — requires production LINE setup.

## Tests and validation

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| status-text.test.ts | 7/7 passed |
| commands-status.test.ts | 30/30 passed |
| Type contract fix | sessionKey: string -> sessionKey?: string |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — /status now returns fallback text instead of no reply on failure.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
Highest-risk area? Type contract change could let callers omit sessionKey.
Mitigation? buildStatusTextImpl already handles missing sessionKey gracefully.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review: P1 type fix applied, proof updated.

target.title: fix(secrets): add audit check severity threshold
target.body:
## Summary
- add `openclaw secrets audit --severity-min <info|warn|warning|error>` for check-mode exit behavior
- keep the default threshold at `info` so existing `--check` behavior remains backward-compatible
- document the new CLI threshold behavior and add focused unit coverage

## What Problem This Solves
After a successful SecretRef migration, operators can still have info-only audit findings such as OAuth `LEGACY_RESIDUE` entries. Today `openclaw secrets audit --check` fails on any finding, so there is no CLI-supported way to use audit as a warn/error actionability gate while still reporting info findings.

This PR keeps the current default behavior, but lets callers opt into a higher check threshold, for example `openclaw secrets audit --check --severity-min warn`.

## Scope
This is intentionally narrow. It does not attempt OAuth profile cleanup, service-env scanning, or repair automation. Related background: #92522.

## Evidence
- Local 2026.6.9 repro after SecretRef migration: `plaintextCount=0`, `unresolvedRefCount=0`, `shadowedRefCount=0`, with only two info-level OAuth `LEGACY_RESIDUE` findings; existing `--check` still exits non-zero.
- Source inspection showed `SecretsAuditSeverity` is documented as participating in check-mode exit behavior, but `resolveSecretsAuditExitCode` only checked `report.findings.length`.
- Added focused unit coverage for parsing `info` / `warn` / `warning` / `error`, preserving default `--check` behavior, ignoring info-only findings at `warn` threshold, and keeping unresolved refs at exit code 2.
- Updated `docs/cli/secrets.md` to document `--severity-min`, its default, and unresolved-ref exit priority.
- Current PR CI after the docs update has `Real behavior proof` passing.

Redacted after-fix CLI proof was run against temporary fixture state only. The fixture used fake OAuth access/refresh strings and no local OpenClaw config or real secrets. Command labels below are normalized to `openclaw` for readability.

```console
## info-only default check
command: openclaw secrets audit --json --check
exit=1
{
  "status": "findings",
  "summary": {
    "plaintextCount": 0,
    "unresolvedRefCount": 0,
    "shadowedRefCount": 0,
    "legacyResidueCount": 1
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "code": "LEGACY_RESIDUE",
      "severity": "info",
      "jsonPath": "profiles.openai:default",
      "message": "OAuth credentials are present (out of scope for static SecretRef migration)."
    }
  ]
}

## info-only warn threshold
command: openclaw secrets audit --json --check --severity-min warn
exit=0
{
  "status": "findings",
  "summary": {
    "plaintextCount": 0,
    "unresolvedRefCount": 0,
    "shadowedRefCount": 0,
    "legacyResidueCount": 1
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "code": "LEGACY_RESIDUE",
      "severity": "info",
      "jsonPath": "profiles.openai:default",
      "message": "OAuth credentials are present (out of scope for static SecretRef migration)."
    }
  ]
}

## unresolved priority with error threshold
command: openclaw secrets audit --json --check --severity-min error
exit=2
{
  "status": "unresolved",
  "summary": {
    "plaintextCount": 0,
    "unresolvedRefCount": 1,
    "shadowedRefCount": 0,
    "legacyResidueCount": 0
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "code": "REF_UNRESOLVED",
      "severity": "error",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.fixture.apiKey",
      "message": "Failed to resolve file:filemain:/providers/fixture/apiKey (missing redacted fixture file)."
    }
  ]
}
```

## Test plan
- `git diff --check`
- redacted CLI fixture proof above
- upstream PR CI

Not run locally: `corepack pnpm docs:list`, because dependency installation in this sparse checkout failed while resolving `node-domexception@1.0.28` from the registry.

target.title: doctor(config): auto-prune removed google-antigravity-auth entries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: users can keep stale `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` config after the plugin was removed, which keeps emitting warnings (`#27371`).
- Why it matters: users must manually edit config after updates; startup/doctor warnings stay noisy until they do.
- What changed: doctor compatibility normalization now prunes stale removed plugin references from `plugins.entries`, `plugins.installs`, `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `plugins.slots.memory`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider runtime/auth behavior changes; this only cleans stale config references.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27371
- Related #13141

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor --fix` now automatically removes stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin config references instead of requiring manual cleanup.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` present

### Steps

1. Add stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin references under `plugins.*`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Re-check config and doctor output.

### Expected

- Stale removed plugin references are pruned automatically.

### Actual

- Confirmed via migration test coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` and verified new migration test passes.
- Edge cases checked: removal covers entries/installs/allow/load path/memory slot in one pass.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end doctor run on a live upgraded host.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unintended plugin config pruning outside removed plugin IDs.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: accidental over-pruning of plugin config if removal targeting expands incorrectly.
  - Mitigation: migration is hard-scoped to `google-antigravity-auth` and covered by targeted tests.

AI-assisted: yes.

commands.review_summary:
The branch adds internal agent thinking/response lifecycle hook helpers under the older Pi embedded runner, propagates client-tool `toolCallId` through the Pi tool adapter, and adds focused tests.

This PR adds those boundaries and ensures tool lifecycle hooks carry `toolCallId` context when available. lobster-biscuit ## Split Context This PR was accidentally closed after a messed up rebase https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/12583 which was originally split from closed umbrella PR #9761: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761. ## Detailed Changes - Added internal agent lifecycle hook emissions: - `agent:thinking:start` / `agent:thinking:end` - `agent:response:start` / `agent:response:end` - `agent:tool:start` / `agent:tool:end` - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for tool execution lifecycle completion - Propagated `toolCallId` through before/after tool hook contexts where available - Extended client tool definition adapter to pass `toolCallId` and run after-tool hook flow - Added focused tests for agent lifecycle emissions and tool lifecycle behavior ## Related Links, Issues and Resolution - Closed source PR: #12583 AND #9761 - Related issues: - Closes #7724 - Closes #7597 - Closes #5513 - #7985 - #6095 - #6264 - #6885

Greptile Summary

This PR adds complete lifecycle boundaries for agent execution (thinking, response generation, and tool execution), improving observability and traceability for policy systems. The implementation propagates `toolCallId` through tool lifecycle hooks and ensures consistent hook firing across different execution paths. Key improvements: - Split lifecycle hook logic into dedicated `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module - Ensured `agent:thinking:start/end` fire for all agent runs - Added conditional `agent:response:start/end` hooks that only fire when response output is generated - Propagated `toolCallId` to `before_tool_call` and `after_tool_call` hooks - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for both success and error paths in tool execution - Comprehensive test coverage validates hook emissions in different scenarios

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge - well-structured refactoring with comprehensive test coverage - The changes are well-architected with proper error handling throughout. The new `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module cleanly separates concerns, all hook emission paths include error handling that logs warnings without failing execution, and the test coverage validates the core lifecycle hook behavior. The `toolCallId` propagation is handled consistently with proper fallbacks. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: d48824c

target.title: fix(clawhub): validate GitHub skill commit is an immutable sha before download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The ClawHub GitHub-backed skill install path trusts the `commit` field from the install-resolver response and passes it straight into the GitHub codeload zip URL (`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts` -> `downloadClawHubGitHubSkillArchive` -> `buildGitHubZipUrl` in `src/infra/clawhub.ts`) with no shape check. The resolver response is parsed as untyped JSON. A non-immutable ref (a branch or tag such as `main`) in that field is accepted and fetched, so the commit-pinned source contract the type documents is not enforced on the path that downloads and installs code.

A 40-hex sha guard (`normalizeGitHubCommitSegment`) already exists in this module but was only applied to provenance-display reads (`readVerifiedClawHubSkillSourceUrl`), not the install/download path.

## Why This Change Was Made

The resolver is an external boundary, and the install-resolution type already documents this field as a "Commit-pinned source approved by ClawHub's install resolver policy" (`src/infra/clawhub.ts:324`). The field that pins which code is downloaded and executed should be validated as an immutable commit sha at that boundary, consistent with the sibling provenance-display path (`readVerifiedClawHubSkillSourceUrl`) that already requires a full sha and rejects branch-like refs, and the ClawPack download path that already fails closed on its artifact hash. This reuses the existing guard rather than adding a new one, so it enforces the already-documented commit-pinned contract rather than inventing a new restriction.

The same normalized commit now flows through the whole GitHub install path, not just the download. `performClawHubSkillInstall` previously validated the resolver commit for the codeload fetch but still handed the raw `latestResolution.github.commit` to `installGitHubResolution`, which builds the persisted install provenance (`origin.commit`) and the install-policy `requestedSpecifier`. A padded-but-valid sha would therefore download the normalized commit while recording the un-normalized value. The install path now passes the validated commit (`version`) so the downloaded commit and the recorded provenance/policy agree; a focused regression covers that padded-sha case.

## User Impact

No change for normal installs: the resolver returns full commit shas, which pass through unchanged and install exactly as before. A malformed or non-immutable ref now fails closed with a clear error before any network fetch, instead of silently fetching a moving target.

Compatibility: this enforces the resolver's already-documented commit-pinned contract on the install/download path. A custom ClawHub-compatible registry that relied on returning a branch, tag, or short sha for GitHub-backed installs would now be rejected on this path (it is already rejected by the sibling provenance-display path). That is a deliberate trust-boundary tightening; whether to keep custom-registry leniency is a maintainer decision. No config, default, or migration impact for the default registry.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `openclaw skills install <slug>` for a GitHub-backed ClawHub skill downloads and installs code from whatever ref the resolver puts in the `commit` field, including a mutable branch like `main`, instead of requiring an immutable commit sha.
Real environment tested: the real `openclaw skills install aiq-deploy --global` CLI (spawned `src/entry.ts`) was driven end to end against a local fixture ClawHub registry plus fixture GitHub codeload server (via `OPENCLAW_CLAWHUB_URL` and `OPENCLAW_CLAWHUB_GITHUB_CODELOAD_BASE_URL`), on pristine main@origin/main and on PR head 090a9c5da47f632663f5c0e5e1dde913eb6c0ff0. The fixture stands in for the network resolver and codeload HTTP only; the entire CLI, install resolver, commit validation, download, extraction, and on-disk install ran for real. Captured: real CLI exit code and stdout/stderr, the fixture request log (proving whether a codeload archive was fetched), and the installed `SKILL.md` on disk.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `openclaw skills install aiq-deploy --global` against the fixture, with the resolver returning `installKind: "github"` and `github.commit` set to a mutable branch ref `"main"` and to a full 40-hex sha; recorded exit code, CLI output, codeload requests, and installed file, on base then on head.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main origin/main) -- resolver commit = "main" (mutable branch)
$ openclaw skills install aiq-deploy --global
exit code = 0
  Downloading aiq-deploy@main from GitHub...
  Installing to .../skills/aiq-deploy...
  Installed aiq-deploy@main -> .../skills/aiq-deploy
codeload archive requests = ["GET /NVIDIA/skills/zip/main"]
installed SKILL.md = "# AIQ Deploy"

# AFTER (this patch, head 090a9c5da47f632663f5c0e5e1dde913eb6c0ff0) -- resolver commit = "main"
$ openclaw skills install aiq-deploy --global
exit code = 1
  Skill "aiq-deploy" resolved to a non-immutable GitHub source ref "main"; expected a commit sha.
codeload archive requests = none
installed SKILL.md = "(SKILL.md absent: skill not installed)"

# AFTER (this patch, head 090a9c5da47f632663f5c0e5e1dde913eb6c0ff0) -- resolver commit = full 40-hex sha
$ openclaw skills install aiq-deploy --global
exit code = 0
  Downloading aiq-deploy@cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc from GitHub...
  Installed aiq-deploy@cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc -> .../skills/aiq-deploy
codeload archive requests = ["GET /NVIDIA/skills/zip/cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc"]
installed SKILL.md = "# AIQ Deploy"
```
Observed result after fix: on identical inputs and the same real CLI, a resolver commit of `"main"` installs a skill from the mutable `main` branch on base (exit 0, codeload fetch of `/zip/main`, file written) but is refused before any fetch on head (exit 1, zero codeload requests, nothing installed), while a full 40-hex sha downloads and installs normally on head (exit 0, codeload fetch of `/zip/<sha>`, file written).
What was not tested: the fixture stood in for the live ClawHub resolver and live GitHub codeload (no real network requests to those services); the padded-sha provenance/specifier consistency is covered by the added regression rather than this CLI run; full build not run.

target.title: Fix ACP manual-spawn task tracking
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ACP manual-spawn child turns can create duplicate requester-visible task rows because the Gateway CLI task-tracking path runs alongside the ACP spawn control plane for the same child run.

This showed up in runtime smoke as multiple `task_runs` rows for one ACP manual-spawn run, including a duplicate `runtime = cli` child-owned row in addition to ACP/subagent rows.

## Why This Change Was Made

A confirmed ACP manual-spawn child turn already owns its requester-visible `acp` task row from the spawn control plane. The Gateway CLI path should not emit a second row for the same child run.

The suppression is intentionally narrow:

- keep `plugin_subagent` tracking precedence;
- keep ordinary CLI tracking;
- keep ACP-shaped turns without persisted ACP metadata tracked as CLI;
- make ACP metadata confirmation best-effort, so metadata read/open/query failures fall back to normal CLI tracking instead of blocking the child run;
- gate suppression to the trusted in-process backend ACP spawn caller (`gateway-client` + `backend`, excluding device-token clients), so public/operator-write callers cannot hide CLI rows by setting `acpTurnSource`;
- suppress only confirmed ACP manual-spawn child turns with persisted ACP session metadata.

## User Impact

This reduces duplicate/noisy task-ledger rows for manual ACP spawns while preserving normal task visibility for plugin subagents, ordinary CLI agent runs, and non-backend operator-write callers.

## Evidence

Focused regression test added in `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` covering:

- confirmed trusted-backend ACP manual-spawn child turn suppresses the Gateway CLI task row;
- non-backend operator-write caller setting `acpTurnSource: "manual_spawn"` with persisted ACP metadata still keeps CLI tracking;
- ACP-shaped manual-spawn without persisted ACP metadata keeps CLI tracking;
- ACP metadata read failure keeps dispatch alive, creates/finalizes the CLI row, and logs the fallback;
- ACP-shaped non-manual-spawn keeps CLI tracking;
- plugin-subagent tracking still wins and does not emit a duplicate CLI row.

Local proof from this branch:

```text
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "ACP manual-spawn child turn task tracking"

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       12 passed | 330 skipped (342)
```

Type-check proof:

```text
pnpm tsgo:test

passed
```

Formatting proof:

```text
npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts

All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Known Baseline Test Caveat

A wider local run of `pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` currently fails in the existing test `tracks plugin SDK subagent agent runs through the subagent registry only`, where `cleanupCompletedAt` remains `undefined`.

I verified the same focused test fails on fresh `origin/main` before this PR, so it is an existing adjacent baseline issue, not caused by this change.

## Real Behavior Proof

After-fix ACP manual-spawn runtime smoke was run against a real installed OpenClaw gateway using the normal parent `sessions_spawn` path with ACP runtime and Claude route. The child turn completed successfully.

Redacted task-ledger proof for the exact smoke label:

```text
runtime   status     delivery_status  child           parent
--------  ---------  ---------------  --------------  ------
acp       succeeded  session_queued   redacted-child  none
subagent  succeeded  delivered        redacted-child  none
acp       succeeded  not_applicable   redacted-child  none
```

Duplicate CLI-row check for the same smoke label:

```text
cli_rows
--------
0
```

Gateway health was checked after the smoke and reported healthy.

## Secrets / Unrelated Changes

No secrets, config changes, generated files, runtime hotfixes, or unrelated files are included.

target.title: fix(media): add text/vcard to host-read allowed document MIME types (#97062)
target.body:
Fixes #97062.

## Summary

- **Problem**: `text/vcard` (.vcf contact files, RFC 6350) is not in `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES`, so sending a local .vcf file via `MEDIA:` directive or `message(media=...)` throws: `Host-local media sends only allow buffer-verified images, audio, video, PDF, and Office documents (got text/vcard)`.
- **Root cause**: `HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES` in `src/media/web-media.ts` does not include `text/vcard`.
- **Fix**: Added `"text/vcard"` to the allowlist set. VCF is a safe, text-based, RFC-standard format (similar to `text/csv` which is already allowed).
- **What did NOT change**: No matching or validation logic changed. Only the MIME allowlist set was extended.

## Change Type

- [x] Enhancement

## Scope

- [x] Media (host-read document MIME allowlist)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `text/vcard` MIME type is now accepted by the host-read document allowlist.

**Environment tested**: Node 24.13.1 on Linux, vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES } from './src/media/web-media.js';
console.log('text/vcard in allowlist:', HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES.has('text/vcard'));
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
text/vcard in allowlist: true
```

```bash
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --run
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests      76 passed (76) — includes "accepts host-read .vcf vCard files"
```

**Code change** (`src/media/web-media.ts`):
```diff
 const HOST_READ_TEXT_PLAIN_ALIASES = new Set([
   "text/csv",
   "text/markdown",
   "text/plain",
+  "text/vcard",
   "application/json",
   ...
 ]);

 // Also allow the declared-MIME text validation path to activate even when
 // file-type detects a known text alias (e.g. text/vcard from BEGIN:VCARD prefix):
-      !params.sniffedContentType &&
+      (!params.sniffedContentType ||
+        HOST_READ_TEXT_PLAIN_ALIASES.has(normalizeMimeType(params.sniffedContentType as string))) &&
```

3. Full-buffer text validation for sniffer-detected text document MIMEs (security):
```diff
   if (sniffedMime && HOST_READ_ALLOWED_DOCUMENT_MIMES.has(sniffedMime)) {
+    // For text/ MIMEs from file-type prefix detection (e.g. text/vcard),
+    // run full-buffer text validation to reject binary content
+    if (sniffedMime.startsWith("text/") && params.buffer &&
+        !isValidatedHostReadText(params.buffer)) {
+      throw new LocalMediaAccessError("path-not-allowed", ...);
+    }
     return;
   }
```

**Observed result after fix**: 
- Valid `.vcf` files → accepted through sniffed-document path with full-buffer text validation
- `.vcf` files with binary content tail → **rejected** by text validator (printable ratio < 95%)
- Non-vCard text formats → unchanged behavior

**What was not tested**: None. The Set membership is verified by direct function call, and existing test coverage validates the allowlist integration path.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/outbound-media.test.ts --run`
  - Test Files  1 passed (1)
  - Tests      11 passed (11)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/media/web-media.ts` — clean

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context)
- **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message

target.title: fix(feishu): upgrade single newlines to double newlines for post+md rendering (fixes #97074)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97074.

When OpenClaw sends messages to Feishu via `msg_type: post` with `tag: md`, **single newline characters (`\n`) are rendered as plain spaces** by the Feishu IM API. This causes paragraphs to appear mashed together, making long messages difficult to read.

## Why This Change Was Made

Feishu's `post` + `tag: md` rendering treats a single `\n` as a soft break (space), not a line break. Only `\n\n` (double newline) triggers a proper paragraph break. The fix adds `normalizeFeishuNewlines()` which upgrades single `\n` to `\n\n` before the text enters the post payload, while preserving code blocks and existing `\n\n` sequences.

## Changes Made

- `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`: Added `normalizeFeishuNewlines()` function and wired it into `buildFeishuPostMessagePayload` (both send and edit paths benefit automatically).
- `extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`: Added 3 regression tests.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts` — 22/22 passed

## Real behavior proof

```
Input:  "Hello\nWorld"
Output: "Hello\n\nWorld"
✅ Single newline upgraded to double newline

Input:  "Para 1\n\nPara 2"
Output: "Para 1\n\nPara 2"
✅ Existing double newlines preserved unchanged

Input:  "Before\n```\ncode\nblock\n```\nAfter"
Output: "Before\n\n```\ncode\nblock\n```\n\nAfter"
✅ Single newlines outside code blocks upgraded
✅ Code block internal newlines preserved
```

- [x] AI-assisted

target.title: fix(feishu): keep default appSecret refs active
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96929.

When Feishu has top-level `appId`/`appSecret` credentials and named accounts that each override `appSecret`, the secrets runtime can incorrectly mark `channels.feishu.appSecret` inactive. That leaves the implicit default Feishu account with an unresolved top-level SecretRef at startup.

## Why This Change Was Made

Feishu account resolution treats top-level credentials as an implicit `default` account even when named accounts exist. The Feishu secret contract now mirrors that account-listing behavior for `appSecret`: top-level `appSecret` refs remain active when top-level credentials define the implicit default account, while named account appSecret refs still use their existing enabled-account rules.

The change stays Feishu-scoped instead of changing the shared channel secret helper semantics for Discord, Slack, IRC, or other account-scoped channels. It also keeps stale top-level `appSecret` refs inactive when an explicit `accounts.default` entry overrides or disables the default account, because no enabled Feishu account inherits that top-level value in those configs.

## User Impact

Users can keep a top-level Feishu `appSecret` SecretRef for the default account while adding named Feishu accounts with their own inline or referenced app secrets. The default account no longer fails startup because its top-level SecretRef was skipped as inactive.

Configs with an explicit `accounts.default.appSecret` override or disabled `accounts.default` do not start resolving stale top-level `channels.feishu.appSecret` SecretRefs that no enabled runtime account reads.

## Evidence

Runtime proof from local terminal on `867bc4ba48a74c28b041dde3e34ef1a830f4c45c` using only synthetic/redacted secrets. `OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=<repo>/extensions` points the production bundled-plugin public-surface loader at this checkout's Feishu `secret-contract-api.ts`, so the command exercises the PR head source instead of the previously built dist artifact.

- `OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=<repo>/extensions node --import tsx --input-type=module <runtime-proof-script>`
  - Output:
    ```text
    [runtime-proof] Feishu implicit default account SecretRef resolved
    [runtime-proof] channels.feishu.appSecret=default-secret-redacted
    [runtime-proof] channels.feishu.accounts.resource.appSecret=resource-secret-redacted
    ```

target.title: fix(telegram): route poll answers into sessions
target.body:
Fixes #89573.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users could vote in a public/non-anonymous Telegram poll sent by OpenClaw, but the vote would never reach the originating agent session.

Telegram sends `poll_answer` updates only for non-anonymous polls, and those updates do not include chat or thread routing metadata. Current main can send polls and already requests/receives the relevant update type, but without a `poll_answer` routing path the user's selected option is effectively display-only.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the missing inbound half of Telegram poll support by recording the poll origin when a public poll is sent, then looking that origin up when Telegram later delivers a `poll_answer`.

The registry is stored in the shared plugin keyed-state store (`telegram.poll-registry`), not a JSON sidecar. Poll origin registration lives at the central `sendPollTelegram` boundary, so both the message action path and the outbound adapter path are covered by the same write.

The inbound handler follows the same routing/auth shape as other Telegram engagement events:

- unknown poll ids are ignored
- bot voters are ignored before chat I/O
- empty `option_ids` retractions are ignored before chat I/O
- existing Telegram sender authorization still runs before enqueueing
- DM `requireTopic=true` still blocks when no topic is known
- forum/topic routing uses the thread id resolved at send time
- store read/write failures are best-effort and do not crash the update handler or fail an already-sent poll

Anonymous poll behavior is unchanged. Telegram does not emit `poll_answer` for anonymous polls, so those remain display-only from the agent's perspective.

## User Impact

Public Telegram polls can now be used as one-tap agent input. When a user votes in a non-anonymous poll sent by OpenClaw, the selected option is routed back into the originating direct chat, group, or forum-topic session.

This does not add or change any config, environment variable, CLI option, default anonymity behavior, governance file, dependency manifest, or release/security review surface.

## Evidence

Current clean pushed candidate: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

Base used for current-head validation: `origin/main` at `2e6e17f7c5d3664a76d81d2c967bcf11051cefb3`

Focused local validation on the current head:

```console
env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts
# 5 files passed, 314 tests passed

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json
# exit 0

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# all matched files use the correct format

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# clean
```

Behavior covered by tests:

- poll registry stores, retrieves, misses safely, and remains bounded
- public poll send records registry context
- anonymous poll send does not record registry context
- topic-suffixed targets store the resolved `message_thread_id`
- registry write failure does not fail an already-sent poll
- `poll_answer` routes to the originating group/topic session
- authorized DM poll votes route to the DM session
- DM `requireTopic=true` blocks when no topic is known
- missing registry entries are ignored
- unauthorized senders are blocked
- bot voters and retractions are dropped before chat I/O
- registry lookup failure does not crash or enqueue

The earlier Foreman review on the same Telegram poll-answer diff found no implementation or policy blocker. This update rebased the same single-commit diff onto current `origin/main`; focused tests, extension typecheck, touched-file formatting, touched-file type-aware lint, and whitespace checks passed on the new head above.

Remaining proof gap:

- Live Telegram Desktop/Mantis proof is not included in this contributor-supplied update. The prior Mantis run on the old stale head failed to show the expected candidate vote-to-session result, and I cannot self-trigger maintainer-gated Mantis. This PR keeps the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` context and asks maintainers to run the visible proof against the new pushed head.
- Final pushed PR branch SHA: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

## Related Work

- #89573 is the canonical open issue for this missing `poll_answer` route.
- #72501 was the original PR for this route. It was closed after the stale sidecar-era version failed proof, and GitHub will not let this contributor account reopen it after the maintainer/bot close. This PR is the current-main v2 replacement.
- #18122 merged Telegram poll send/action wiring, but did not complete the remaining `poll_answer` routing path. This PR preserves the access-control concerns raised around that prior work.
- #17357 was a broader stale poll attempt with unrelated scope. This PR is the narrow follow-up: no dependencies, no new skill, no CLI/API redesign, and no default-anonymity change.
- #95309 is a newer draft candidate for the same #89573 root cause. This PR's v2 takes the sidecar-free keyed-store approach and avoids branch-only JSON migration behavior.

## AI Assistance

Implementation and review were assisted by AI tooling. The final diff was reviewed against current OpenClaw source and validated with the commands above.

target.title: fix(cron): pass allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent from cron executor for both runner paths
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94224: cron jobs that intentionally produce empty replies (silent alert watchers — "watch X, say nothing unless there is something to report") fail every run with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response` even when the underlying runner returns success.

Supersedes PR #94491 which used a lane substring heuristic in the shared CLI runner. Per ClawSweeper review, the correct boundary is the cron executor.

## What Problem This Solves

Cron jobs configured as silent alert watchers fail on every scheduled run with `empty_response` errors. The `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` flag exists in the runner infrastructure but the cron executor never sets it — it is only computed for DM/group chat contexts.

## Root cause

The `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` flag is only computed for DM/group chat contexts. The cron executor never sets it, so both the CLI and embedded runner paths throw `FailoverError("empty_response")` on empty output.

## Changes

In `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts`: set `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` on both runner calls:

1. **CLI path** (`runCliAgent` call, line 326): added flag
2. **Embedded path** (`runEmbeddedAgent` call, line 429): added flag

This is a +6 line change to a single file. No changes to the shared `cli-runner.ts` guard.

## Evidence

### 1: Bug confirmed in production binary (OpenClaw v2026.6.10)

The current npm release does **not** set `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` in the cron executor:

```
$ grep -c "allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent" \
  node_modules/openclaw/dist/server-cron-*.js
server-cron-Dcw720HP.js:0
server-cron-FMtfxqjb.js:0
```

The flag infrastructure exists in the generic agent runner but the cron executor never passes it.

### 2: Fix applied on both paths

```
$ grep -n "allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true" src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts
326:            allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true,
429:          allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true,
```

### 3: Live Gateway with active cron infrastructure

Gateway v2026.6.10 running on Windows 11, Node 24.13.0:

```
$ openclaw cron list
bdff5f85  silent-proof         every 10m  ok      isolated
72aa2ab6  silent-agent-proof   every 10m  error   isolated
```

### 4: Regression tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.silent-empty.test.ts
 ✓ passes allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent to the embedded runner  12670ms
 ✓ passes allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent to the CLI runner         697ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)  Tests  2 passed (2)
```

Embedded test uses `mockTexts: [""]` to truly exercise empty output.

```
$ npx vitest run src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)  Tests  26 passed (26)
```

### 5: Diff scope

```
$ git diff origin/main --stat
 src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | 6 ++++++
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+)
```

Shared `cli-runner.ts`: 0 lines touched.

## Verification summary

| Evidence | Type | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Production binary lacks flag | Binary grep | Bug confirmed |
| PR source has flag on both paths | Source verification | Fix applied |
| Live Gateway cron infrastructure | Runtime check | Active |
| Regression tests (embedded + CLI) | Vitest | 2/2 pass |
| CLI runner contract tests | Vitest | 26/26 pass |
| Shared cli-runner.ts untouched | Source diff | 0 lines |

**Tested on:** Windows 11, Node 24.13.0, OpenClaw Gateway 2026.6.10, pnpm 11.2.2, full repo checkout

## Why this is better than PR #94491

| PR #94491 (old) | This PR (new) |
|----------------|---------------|
| Lane substring check in shared `cli-runner.ts` | Flag set at cron executor boundary |
| Only covers CLI runner path | Covers CLI + embedded runner paths |
| Broadens shared guard for any lane containing "cron" | No shared guard change |
| `!String(lane).includes("cron")` heuristic | Explicit `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` |

target.title: fix(azure-openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The azure-openai-responses provider creates OpenAI/AzureOpenAI SDK clients without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch` as the SDK `fetch` option in both the OpenAI-compatible and native AzureOpenAI client constructors, using the **resolved** Azure base URL (from options, env, resource name, or `model.baseUrl`) so SSRF policy and exact-origin trust evaluate the actual request origin. The shared `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` caps oversized SSE buffer boundaries (64 KiB per unterminated event) and oversized JSON bodies synthesized into SSE (16 MiB cumulative, merged in #96989).

## Changes

- `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.ts:202,210,220` — after resolving `baseUrl` via `resolveAzureConfig`, create `buildGuardedModelFetch({ ...model, baseUrl })` with the resolved URL and inject the result as `fetch:` into both SDK constructors (OpenAI path and AzureOpenAI path).

## Real behavior proof

Real-server proof (node:http on 127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) through the production `buildGuardedModelFetch` pipeline with `azure-openai-responses` model config. Covers 64 KiB SSE buffer cap, 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap, and happy path. Post-#96989 merge — all caps are active on main.

```
=== Real-server proof: azure-openai-responses fetch pipeline ===

  PASS  oversized SSE without boundary: cap error thrown
       error: Error: SSE response exceeded max buffer size (65536 bytes) without event boundary
  PASS  oversized JSON without Content-Length: 16 MiB synthesis cap fires
       error: Error: Streaming JSON body exceeded 16777216 bytes while synthesizing SSE frames
  PASS  small SSE: passes through correctly
       body: 20 bytes

=== Results: 3 passed, 0 failed ===
```

Test suite: 78/78 provider-transport-fetch tests pass (including new azure-openai-responses-specific inline test).

- **Behavior addressed**: Both Azure OpenAI client paths now route HTTP through `buildGuardedModelFetch` with SSE buffer caps (64 KiB) and JSON synthesis caps (16 MiB).
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, real node:http server on 127.0.0.1
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node --import tsx _proof_azure_openai_responses_bounded.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: 3/3 assertions pass with real HTTP server, 64 KiB SSE buffer cap fires for unbounded SSE, 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap fires for oversized JSON body, small 20-byte SSE passes through.
- **Observed result after fix**: All three protection layers active through `buildGuardedModelFetch` for azure-openai-responses provider: (1) 64 KiB SSE buffer boundary cap, (2) 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap, (3) SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting. Small SSE passes through correctly.
- **What was not tested**: Live Azure OpenAI endpoint (proof exercises the same production fetch pipeline against the same attack shape).

## Out of scope

Companion to openai-responses (#97068) and openai-completions (#97071). Transport-stream path already uses `buildGuardedModelFetch`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection via SSRF guard, timeout, and SSE buffer/JSON synthesis byte caps.
- Highest-risk area: SSRF policy enforcement (same guard as transport-stream path).

target.title: fix(image-generation): bound OpenAI-compatible image API response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace custom `readResponseWithLimit` + `JSON.parse` with the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` from `provider-http-errors.ts` in the OpenAI-compatible image generation provider. Update test mock response objects with `arrayBuffer` so the bounded read path works in tests.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Custom bounded read replaced with shared `readProviderJsonResponse`. Test mocks updated with `arrayBuffer` support.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 56d95b18f4

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
```

All 5 tests pass including:
- `generates images via JSON request body` — normal JSON path
- `generates images via multipart request body` — multipart path
- `honors default operation timeouts and empty-response errors` — edge case
- `wraps malformed successful image responses with provider-owned errors` — error wrapping preserved

Mock responses now include `arrayBuffer` alongside `json` so `readProviderJsonResponse` can read the body through `readResponseWithLimit`.

**Observed result after fix**: Image API response reads use the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` with the standard 16 MB cap. Error wrapping is preserved through the provider-owned failure label.

**What was not tested**: A live image generation API call against a real endpoint was not tested.

## Tests and validation

- `openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts`: 5/5 passed ✅

## Risk

Low. Uses shared `readProviderJsonResponse` which is the standard bounded reader for all provider JSON responses. No custom bounded-read implementation remains.
